deep_dive.title: ConsisFormer: Compute-Efficient Transformer for Wireless Foundation Models Based on Channel Consistency
deep_dive.abstract: Wireless foundation models (WFMs) have recently emerged as a promising paradigm for AI-native 6G networks, enabling universal channel representations adaptable to diverse communication and sensing tasks. Existing WFMs are predominantly built upon the Transformer architecture, which delivers superior performance but incurs computational complexity proportional to the square of the input sequence length, posing a significant barrier to their deployment under stringent inference latency constraints. To address this issue, in this paper, we propose ConsisFormer, a compute-efficient Transformer design based on short-term consistency of wireless channels, as a WFM backbone. By utilizing the observation that adjacent time or frequency instances share similar clusters of scatterers and thus exhibit similar channel characteristics, we develop an adaptive token aggregation (ATA) module to dynamically merge neighboring channel state information (CSI) tokens, thereby reducing the length of the token sequence involved in self-attention calculations to lower the computational cost. Furthermore, we propose a feature sequence interpolation (FSI) method to recover the full CSI representation based on the sparse feature sequence outputted from the Transformer blocks, thus keeping the performance unaffected while ensuring low complexity. Moreover, we propose an aggregated auto-encoder (AAE) pre-training paradigm for WFMs, enabling robust channel representation learning from sparsified CSI tokens via compression and recovery. Simulation results show that the proposed design reduces the computational complexity of WFM by over $83\%$ with negligible performance loss on various tasks including channel prediction, LoS/NLOS classification, beam prediction, and localization.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于无线信道的计算高效Transformer
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于无线信道的计算高效Transformer
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于无线信道的计算高效Transformer
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes ConsisFormer, a compute-efficient transformer exploiting channel consistency for wireless foundation models, reducing quadratic complexity.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对无线基础模型中Transformer计算复杂度高的瓶颈，提出ConsisFormer架构。该架构利用无线信道短时一致性，设计高效注意力机制，将复杂度从输入长度的平方降为线性。在无线信道建模和感知任务上，ConsisFormer在保持性能的同时显著降低推理延迟，适用于6G AI原生网络。

deep_dive.title: Site-Specific MIMO Channel Generation via Diffusion and Flow Matching: Fidelity, Efficiency, and Downstream Utility
deep_dive.abstract: This paper explores the use of generative models to synthesize high-quality, site-specific multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) channel data, addressing the high cost of the extensive measurement campaigns required to acquire real-world data for AI-native wireless networks. Two location-conditioned generative paradigms are compared: a conditional denoising diffusion implicit model (cDDIM), and a conditional flow matching model (cFMM). Both these models generate MIMO channel matrices conditioned on user coordinates, to preserve the spatial structure of the deployment site. The approaches are evaluated across three dimensions: statistical fidelity (including beam consistency and effective rank), generation efficiency, and utility in downstream tasks such as channel-state information compression and beam alignment. Results across diverse propagation scenarios (28 GHz and 3.5 GHz, both line-of-sight and non-line-of-sight) demonstrate that both models accurately capture site-specific characteristics, even when trained on scarce ground-truth data. Notably, cFMM achieves a quality comparable to cDDIM with roughly an order of magnitude less inference time. Augmenting scarce site-specific datasets with these synthetic channels yields hefty performance gains in downstream physical layer tasks compared to using scarce data alone or stochastic channels.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: uses generative models (diffusion, flow matching) for MIMO channel generation, directly applying neural networks to channel modeling
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用扩散模型和流匹配生成MIMO信道，直接应用神经网络于信道建模
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用扩散模型和流匹配生成MIMO信道，直接应用神经网络于信道建模
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用扩散模型和流匹配生成MIMO信道，直接应用神经网络于信道建模
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Compares cDDIM and cFMM for site-specific MIMO channel generation, achieving high statistical fidelity and downstream utility.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对真实信道数据获取成本高昂的问题，探索使用条件扩散模型和流匹配模型生成特定场地MIMO信道数据。两种模型均以用户坐标为条件，保留空间结构。评估表明，生成数据在统计保真度（波束一致性、有效秩）和下游效用方面表现优异，可有效替代实测数据。

deep_dive.title: Quantum-classical physics-informed Kolmogorov-Arnold networks for PDEs
deep_dive.abstract: We develop QCPIKAN, the first quantum-classical physics-informed Kolmogorov-Arnold network designed to solve partial differential equations (PDEs). Built upon Chebyshev-polynomial KAN layers and parameterized quantum circuits, this hybrid framework embeds physical constraints into the training loss to enforce physical consistency. Our theoretical investigations grounded in approximation theory prove that this design accelerates high-frequency error convergence to an exponential rate and effectively mitigates numerical dispersion. We validate the framework across three typical seepage scenarios in porous media, including single-phase flow, component transport and two-phase flow. Compared with existing quantum-classical physics-informed neural networks, QCPIKAN achieves superior performance in global prediction accuracy, local error control, dynamic evolution tracking and displacement front localization. This work provides a robust and efficient alternative for solving complex PDEs.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: quantum-classical physics-informed Kolmogorov-Arnold network for solving PDEs, directly relevant to KAN and PINN in communications
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 量子经典物理信息KAN网络求解偏微分方程，与通信中KAN和PINN直接相关
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 量子经典物理信息KAN网络求解偏微分方程，与通信中KAN和PINN直接相关
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 量子经典物理信息KAN网络求解偏微分方程，与通信中KAN和PINN直接相关
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Develops QCPIKAN, a hybrid quantum-classical physics-informed Kolmogorov-Arnold network that embeds PDE constraints into loss. Proves exponential convergence of high-frequency errors. Validated on seepage flows, demonstrating superiority over classical PINNs.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对物理信息神经网络在求解偏微分方程时高频误差收敛慢的问题，该论文提出首个量子经典物理信息Kolmogorov-Arnold网络（QCPIKAN）。它结合Chebyshev多项式KAN层和参数化量子电路，将物理约束嵌入损失函数。理论证明可加速高频误差指数收敛并抑制数值色散。在多孔介质渗流场景（单相流、组分输运、两相流）中验证，性能优于现有量子经典PINN。该方法将KAN与PINN融合，为通信信道建模中利用物理先验提供了新颖且高效的神经网络架构。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对物理信息神经网络在求解偏微分方程时高频误差收敛慢的问题，该论文提出首个量子经典物理信息Kolmogorov-Arnold网络（QCPIKAN）。它结合Chebyshev多项式KAN层和参数化量子电路，将物理约束嵌入损失函数。理论证明可加速高频误差指数收敛并抑制数值色散。在多孔介质渗流场景（单相流、组分输运、两相流）中验证，性能优于现有量子经典PINN。该方法将KAN与PINN融合，为通信信道建模中利用物理先验提供了新颖且高效的神经网络架构。

quick_skim.title: Physics-Informed Neural Network with Squeeze-Excitation-like Attention
quick_skim.abstract: We introduce SEA-PINN, a novel architecture that incorporates a Squeeze-Excitation-like attention mechanism into physics-informed neural networks to dynamically recalibrate the importance of neurons across layers. A key feature of SEA-PINN is its highly stable initialization. On 17 out of 20 benchmark problems, SEA-PINN exhibit nearly negligible variance and significantly reduced initial loss, establishing a quasi-deterministic and favorable starting point for optimization. Notably, without employing Fourier feature embeddings or periodic activation functions, SEA-PINN attained competitive accuracy (83\% vs. 90\% improvement relative to FNN-PINN on the high-frequency case 7) as compared with TSA-PINN-a model specifically engineered for high-frequency problems via learnable frequencies in sinusoidal activations. Furthermore, integrating SEA-PINN into TSA-PINN boosted performance by 42.49\%. These results underscore SEA-PINN as a lightweight plug-in module that enhances nonlinear representation power, promotes more robust and efficient convergence, and strengthens the overall reliability of physics-informed learning.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Physics-Informed Neural Network with attention mechanism, relevant to PINN in communication
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 物理信息神经网络引入注意力机制，与通信中的PINN需求相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 物理信息神经网络引入注意力机制，与通信中的PINN需求相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 物理信息神经网络引入注意力机制，与通信中的PINN需求相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: SEA-PINN adds Squeeze-Excitation attention to PINNs, achieving stable initialization and competitive accuracy on high-frequency problems, offering a novel architecture transferable to communication channel modeling.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有物理信息神经网络（PINN）在初始化不稳定和高频问题上面临挑战。本文提出SEA-PINN，在PINN中引入挤压-激励注意力机制，动态调整神经元重要性，实现准确定性的稳定初始化。在20个基准测试中，17个表现出极低方差和显著降低的初始损失，无需傅里叶特征嵌入即可达到与专用高频模型相当的精度。该架构可作为通信信道建模中PINN的改进方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有物理信息神经网络（PINN）在初始化不稳定和高频问题上面临挑战。本文提出SEA-PINN，在PINN中引入挤压-激励注意力机制，动态调整神经元重要性，实现准确定性的稳定初始化。在20个基准测试中，17个表现出极低方差和显著降低的初始损失，无需傅里叶特征嵌入即可达到与专用高频模型相当的精度。该架构可作为通信信道建模中PINN的改进方案。
